overview.purpose:
Build an MCP server that acts as an ACP client to GitHub Copilot's ACP server. This allows Claude Code (as the MCP client) to orchestrate GitHub Copilot through standardized tool calls — delegating tasks, chaining agent workflows, and comparing outputs between Claude and Copilot on the same prompts.
The server bridges two protocols: - Upstream (toward Claude Code): MCP (Model Context Protocol) — exposes tools - Downstream (toward Copilot): ACP (Agent Client Protocol) — sends prompts, manages sessions

goals.goal: Enable Claude Code to delegate specific coding tasks to Copilot via MCP tools
Support agent chaining where Claude plans and Copilot executes (or vice versa)
Enable side-by-side output comparison by running the same prompt through Copilot
Manage Copilot ACP sessions (one-shot and persistent) transparently
Auto-approve all Copilot permission requests (YOLO mode)

existing-solution.name: MCACP
existing-solution.description: General-purpose MCP-to-ACP bridge that works with ANY ACP agent (Copilot, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, etc.). Exposes 22 MCP tools covering registry management, agent lifecycle, session management, agent interaction, and status monitoring. Supports 4 permission policies (allow_all, deny_all, elicit, operator).
existing-solution.differentiation: mcp-copilot-acp differentiates by being purpose-built for the Claude Code + GitHub Copilot workflow. Fewer tools (focused surface), simpler configuration, zero-config startup, and optimized for the single-agent Copilot use case rather than multi-agent orchestration.

competitive-landscape.note: ACP-MCP-Server (GongRzhe) bridges IBM's Agent Communication Protocol — a different protocol also abbreviated "ACP". Not relevant to Agent Client Protocol.

in-scope.item: MCP server exposing Copilot orchestration tools to Claude Code
ACP client implementation connecting to `copilot --acp` via stdio or TCP
Session management: create, reuse, destroy Copilot ACP sessions
Streaming response collection from Copilot ACP updates
Auto-approval of all Copilot permission/tool-execution requests
Working directory passthrough (Copilot works in the same project context)

out-of-scope.item: GUI or web dashboard — CLI/MCP only
Multi-agent orchestration beyond Copilot (Gemini, Codex, etc.) — future extension
Copilot authentication management — assumes `copilot` CLI is already authenticated
Custom permission policies — YOLO mode only for v1
Proxying Copilot's model as an LLM endpoint (this is orchestration, not API proxying)

item.description: Connect to Copilot's ACP server via stdio (`copilot --acp`) by spawning it as a child process, or via TCP (`copilot --acp --port {port}`).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Successfully sends ACP `initialize` and receives `InitializeResponse`
Handles both stdio and TCP transport modes via configuration

item.description: Implement ACP initialization handshake: send `initialize` with client capabilities (fileSystem read/write, terminal create), receive and store agent capabilities from Copilot.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Exchanges protocol version and capabilities per ACP spec
Stores Copilot's advertised capabilities for capability-aware tool exposure

item.description: Create new ACP sessions via `session/new` with configurable working directory that defaults to the current project root.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Returns a valid sessionId from Copilot
Working directory is passed and respected by Copilot

item.description: Send prompts to Copilot via `session/prompt` and collect the full streamed response by aggregating `session/update` notifications until a `PromptResponse` with a stop reason is received.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Sends text prompts with optional context/resources
Aggregates all message chunks into a complete response
Captures tool calls, tool results, and thought updates
Returns structured result with full text, tool calls log, and stop reason

item.description: Auto-approve all Copilot permission requests (`session/request_permission`) by immediately responding with approval. No user interaction required.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Every permission request receives an immediate "allow" response
Permission requests are logged for transparency

item.description: Support two session modes: - One-shot: Create session, send prompt, collect response, destroy session. - Persistent: Create session once, send multiple prompts over time, explicitly destroy when done. Claude Code selects the mode per tool call.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: One-shot tool creates and tears down session automatically
Persistent session tools allow create/prompt/destroy as separate calls
Session state tracked in memory with sessionId mapping

item.description: Handle Copilot's file system requests (`fs/read_text_file`, `fs/write_text_file`) by performing the actual file I/O on the local filesystem, scoped to the session's working directory.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Read requests return file contents from disk
Write requests create/overwrite files on disk
Paths are resolved relative to working directory, no escaping allowed

item.description: Expose MCP tool: `copilot_prompt` — One-shot prompt. Creates a session, sends the prompt, collects the full response, destroys the session, returns the result. Parameters: prompt (string), workingDirectory (string, optional), context (string, optional — additional files or context to include).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Claude Code can call this tool and receive Copilot's complete response
Response includes full text, tool calls performed, and stop reason

item.description: Expose MCP tool: `copilot_session_create` — Creates a persistent Copilot session. Returns a sessionHandle that can be used in subsequent calls. Parameters: workingDirectory (string, optional), name (string, optional).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Returns a sessionHandle identifier
Session remains alive for subsequent prompts

item.description: Expose MCP tool: `copilot_session_prompt` — Sends a prompt to an existing persistent session. Parameters: sessionHandle (string), prompt (string), context (string, optional).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Sends prompt to the correct existing session
Maintains conversation context from prior prompts in that session
Returns same structured result as copilot_prompt

item.description: Expose MCP tool: `copilot_session_destroy` — Tears down a persistent session and cleans up resources. Parameters: sessionHandle (string).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Session is removed from tracking
Copilot process resources are cleaned up if no sessions remain

item.description: Expose MCP tool: `copilot_compare` — Runs the same prompt through Copilot and returns the result alongside a placeholder for Claude's own response (Claude Code handles its side; this tool only provides Copilot's output). Parameters: prompt (string), workingDirectory (string, optional).

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Returns Copilot's response clearly labeled for comparison
Includes metadata: model used (if available), token count, duration

item.description: Expose MCP tool: `copilot_status` — Returns current state: whether the Copilot ACP process is running, active sessions, and Copilot's advertised capabilities.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Reports process alive/dead status
Lists active sessions with their IDs and creation times
Shows Copilot's capabilities from initialization

item.description: Lazily start the Copilot ACP process on first tool call. Do not spawn Copilot at MCP server startup — wait until actually needed.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: No Copilot process running until first tool invocation
First tool call transparently starts and initializes Copilot
Subsequent calls reuse the existing Copilot process

item.description: Automatically restart the Copilot ACP process if it crashes or becomes unresponsive, with a configurable retry limit.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: Detects process exit or unresponsive state (no response within timeout)
Restarts Copilot and re-initializes up to N times (default: 3)
Returns clear error to Claude Code if retries exhausted

item.description: Gracefully shut down: when the MCP server receives shutdown, clean up all Copilot sessions and terminate the Copilot ACP process.

acceptance-criteria.criterion: All active sessions are destroyed before process termination
Copilot child process is terminated cleanly

item.description: Prompt round-trip overhead (excluding Copilot's own thinking time) should be minimal — protocol marshaling adds no more than 100ms.
Server must handle Copilot producing unexpectedly large responses or long-running operations without hanging. Configurable timeout per prompt.
item.metric: Default timeout: 300 seconds per prompt, configurable

item.description: Zero-config startup for the common case: `npx copilot-acp-mcp` or similar. All defaults should work out of the box if `copilot` CLI is on PATH and authenticated.
item.metric: Working setup in under 2 minutes from install

item.description: Structured logging of all ACP messages (sent/received) at debug level for troubleshooting. Errors and permission auto-approvals logged at info level.
item.metric: All ACP traffic loggable via --verbose flag

item.description: Must work on Windows 11 with PowerShell as primary environment. Also support macOS and Linux for portability.
item.metric: All tests pass on Windows 11 (PowerShell 7+), macOS, Ubuntu

architecture.description:
The server sits between Claude Code (MCP client) and Copilot (ACP agent):
┌─────────────┐ MCP (stdio) ┌──────────────────┐ ACP (stdio/TCP) ┌─────────────┐ │ Claude Code │ ◄──────────────────► │ copilot-acp-mcp │ ◄──────────────────────► │ Copilot CLI │ │ (MCP Client)│ tool calls/results │ (MCP Server + │ JSON-RPC 2.0 │ (ACP Agent) │ └─────────────┘ │ ACP Client) │ session/prompt/update └─────────────┘ └──────────────────┘
The MCP server is a single Node.js/TypeScript process that: 1. Listens for MCP tool calls from Claude Code via stdio 2. Translates them into ACP protocol messages 3. Manages the Copilot child process lifecycle 4. Tracks sessions in memory 5. Handles ACP bidirectional communication (Copilot can request files, permissions)

component.name: MCP Server Layer
component.responsibility: Exposes MCP tools (copilot_prompt, copilot_session_*, copilot_compare, copilot_status). Handles MCP protocol via @modelcontextprotocol/sdk.
component.interfaces: MCP stdio transport — tool definitions and call handlers

component.name: ACP Client Layer
component.responsibility: Manages the JSON-RPC 2.0 connection to Copilot's ACP server. Sends requests (initialize, session/new, session/prompt), handles responses, and processes notifications (session/update). Also handles incoming requests FROM Copilot (fs/read_text_file, session/request_permission).
component.interfaces: JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio (child process stdin/stdout) or TCP socket. Bidirectional — both sides can initiate requests.

component.name: Session Manager
component.responsibility: Tracks active sessions, maps sessionHandles (user-facing) to ACP sessionIds (protocol-level). Manages session lifecycle and cleanup.
component.interfaces: Internal — used by MCP tool handlers

component.name: Process Manager
component.responsibility: Spawns and manages the Copilot CLI child process. Handles lazy start, crash detection, restart logic, and graceful shutdown.
component.interfaces: Internal — spawns `copilot --acp` or connects to TCP port

component.name: Response Aggregator
component.responsibility: Collects streaming session/update notifications during a prompt turn. Aggregates message chunks, tool calls, thoughts, and plan updates into a single structured response once the PromptResponse arrives.
component.interfaces: Internal — fed by ACP Client, consumed by MCP tool handlers

data-flow.description:
Example flow for `copilot_prompt` (one-shot):
1. Claude Code calls MCP tool `copilot_prompt` with prompt text 2. MCP Server Layer receives the call 3. Process Manager ensures Copilot is running (lazy start if needed) 4. ACP Client sends `session/new` → receives sessionId 5. ACP Client sends `session/prompt` with the text 6. Copilot streams back `session/update` notifications: - message chunks (agent text) - tool calls (file edits, terminal commands) - permission requests → auto-approved immediately - file read requests → fulfilled from local filesystem 7. Response Aggregator collects all updates 8. Copilot sends `PromptResponse` with stop_reason 9. Session Manager destroys the session 10. MCP Server Layer returns structured result to Claude Code

pattern.name: Lazy Initialization
pattern.rationale: Don't pay Copilot startup cost unless actually needed. First tool call triggers spawn + ACP initialize handshake.

pattern.name: Request-ID Correlation
pattern.rationale: ACP is bidirectional — both sides send requests. Must maintain separate ID counters and correlate responses by ID. Use a pending-requests map with Promise resolvers keyed by request ID.

pattern.name: Notification Routing
pattern.rationale: session/update notifications have no request ID. Route them to the correct Response Aggregator instance by sessionId extracted from the notification params.

decision.question: What language/runtime for the MCP server?

alternatives.alternative: Python with FastMCP — good ecosystem but less native MCP SDK support
Rust — fast but overkill for a bridge server

decision.rationale: The MCP SDK (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk) is TypeScript-native. The ACP protocol is JSON-RPC which is trivial in Node. Child process management is well-supported. B already works with Node/TypeScript in their stack.

decision.question: Stdio vs TCP for connecting to Copilot?
decision.chosen: Default to stdio (spawn child process), with TCP as a config option

alternatives.alternative: TCP only — requires user to start Copilot separately
Stdio only — can't connect to an already-running Copilot

decision.rationale: Stdio is zero-config: we spawn `copilot --acp` ourselves and own the lifecycle. TCP is useful for advanced cases (shared Copilot instance, remote machine). Supporting both is low cost.

decision.question: How to handle Copilot's file system requests?
decision.chosen: Fulfill them directly from local filesystem, scoped to working directory

alternatives.alternative: Reject all fs requests — limits Copilot's ability to read context
Proxy to Claude Code — adds complexity, Claude Code doesn't expose this via MCP

decision.rationale: Copilot needs to read files to understand context. Since we're in YOLO mode, fulfilling fs requests directly is the simplest path. Path traversal prevention is the only guard needed.

decision.question: How to distribute the MCP server to users?
decision.chosen: npm package distributed via npx -y mcp-copilot-acp

alternatives.alternative: Global npm install — version management burden on users
Docker container — overkill for a local bridge tool, slower startup
Standalone CLI binary — more complex build/release pipeline

decision.rationale: npx -y is the standard MCP server distribution pattern (used by the majority of 29,000+ MCP servers on npm). Zero-friction: users always get the latest version. One-command Claude Code setup: claude mcp add --transport stdio copilot-bridge -- npx -y mcp-copilot-acp On Windows: cmd /c npx -y mcp-copilot-acp

decision.question: How to validate MCP tool input schemas?

alternatives.alternative: Manual validation — error-prone, no type inference
JSON Schema directly — verbose, no runtime type narrowing

decision.rationale: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk v1.27+ requires Zod as a peer dependency. Tool input schemas are defined as Zod schemas and automatically converted to JSON Schema for the MCP protocol. Provides compile-time types and runtime validation in one declaration.

decision.question: Permission policy model for Copilot tool approvals?
decision.chosen: allow_all (YOLO mode) as default, with future extensibility for deny_all and elicit

alternatives.alternative: Hardcoded allow_all only — simplest but limits future use
Full 4-policy model from day one — over-engineering for v1

decision.rationale: v1 targets YOLO mode (auto-approve everything). However, the permission handler should be a pluggable interface so deny_all and elicit policies can be added later without refactoring. MCACP's 4-policy model validates that users eventually want granular control.

dependency.name: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk
dependency.purpose: MCP server framework — tool registration, stdio transport, protocol handling
dependency.note: Use v1.x stable. v2 is pre-alpha as of March 2026 — do not adopt yet.

dependency.name: zod
dependency.purpose: Runtime schema validation for MCP tool inputs. Required peer dep of MCP SDK.
dependency.note: MCP SDK accepts ^3.25 || ^4.0. Pin to v3.x for stability. Zod 4.x (4.3.6) is available but v3.x is safer.

dependency.name: cross-spawn
dependency.purpose: Cross-platform child process spawning for copilot --acp
dependency.note: Handles Windows cmd.exe quirks, argument escaping, and PATHEXT resolution.

dependency.name: vitest
dependency.purpose: Test runner — fast, native ESM support, TypeScript out of the box

project-config.tsconfig: target: ES2022 module: NodeNext moduleResolution: NodeNext strict: true esModuleInterop: true skipLibCheck: true forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true outDir: ./dist rootDir: ./src

phase.name: ACP Client Core
phase.description: Build the raw ACP client: JSON-RPC transport, message parsing, request/response correlation, notification handling. Test against `copilot --acp` directly.

milestone.deliverable: Working ACP client that can initialize, create sessions, and send prompts to Copilot

task.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 transport layer (stdio + TCP) with newline-delimited JSON
Request/response correlation with pending-requests map and Promise resolvers
ACP message types: initialize, session/new, session/prompt, session/update parsing
Bidirectional handler for incoming Copilot requests (fs/read_text_file, request_permission)
Response Aggregator: collect session/update chunks into structured result
Integration test: initialize → session/new → session/prompt → collect response from live Copilot

phase.name: MCP Server + Tool Surface
phase.description: Wire the ACP client into an MCP server. Define and implement all MCP tools. Add session management and process lifecycle.

milestone.deliverable: MCP server installable via npx that Claude Code can use to orchestrate Copilot

task.description: MCP server scaffold with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, stdio transport
Process Manager: lazy start, crash detection, restart with retry limit
Session Manager: handle-to-ID mapping, cleanup on destroy

phase.name: Polish + Distribution
phase.description: Configuration, logging, documentation, npm packaging, and Claude Code integration instructions.

milestone.deliverable: Published npm package with README and Claude Code setup guide

task.description: Configuration: env vars and CLI flags for transport mode, port, timeout, retry limit, verbose logging
Structured logging with --verbose flag for ACP message tracing
README with Claude Code MCP configuration examples (.claude/settings.json)
npm package.json, bin entry, tsconfig, build scripts

risk.description: Copilot's ACP implementation is in public preview and may change or have undocumented behaviors. The protocol surface could shift.
risk.mitigation: Pin to a known working Copilot CLI version. Isolate ACP message handling behind an abstraction so changes are contained. Log all raw messages for debugging.

risk.description: Copilot may not support YOLO mode via ACP — the `--yolo` flag reportedly doesn't work with `--acp`. Permission requests may be mandatory.
risk.mitigation: Auto-approve is implemented at the ACP client level (our code responds immediately to permission requests). We don't depend on Copilot's --yolo flag.

risk.description: Copilot CLI may have authentication or license requirements that prevent headless/automated usage via ACP.
risk.mitigation: Validate early in Phase 1. Document required Copilot subscription tier. Fail fast with clear error message if authentication is missing.

risk.description: Windows-specific child process spawning and stdio piping quirks (PowerShell vs cmd, line endings, encoding).
risk.mitigation: Use cross-spawn or execa for child process management. Test on Windows early and often. Handle \r\n line endings in JSON-RPC parsing.

resources.resource: Node.js 22 LTS (22.22.0+), npm, GitHub Copilot CLI (authenticated), TypeScript 5.9+

test.approach: Test encoding/decoding of all ACP message types with known payloads

test.description: Response Aggregator correctly assembles chunks into structured output
test.approach: Feed mock session/update notifications and verify final aggregated result

test.description: Permission auto-approval responds correctly to all permission request variants
test.approach: Mock permission requests with various tool kinds and verify immediate approval response

test.description: Session Manager tracks sessions correctly (create, lookup, destroy)
test.approach: Unit test session CRUD operations and handle-to-ID mapping

test.description: File system handler resolves paths safely within working directory
test.approach: Test with normal paths, relative paths, and path traversal attempts (../ etc)

test.description: Full ACP handshake and one-shot prompt against live Copilot CLI
test.approach: Spawn real Copilot --acp, initialize, create session, send simple prompt, verify response

test.description: MCP tool calls work end-to-end through the server to Copilot
test.approach: Use MCP SDK test client to call each tool and verify responses
Call tool (triggers lazy start), kill Copilot process, call tool again (triggers restart)

test.description: Claude Code delegates a real coding task to Copilot via copilot_prompt

steps.step: Configure MCP server in Claude Code settings
Ask Claude Code to delegate a task to Copilot
Verify Copilot's response is returned and usable by Claude Code

test.expected-result: Claude Code receives and can act on Copilot's coding output

steps.step: Send follow-up prompt referencing prior context
Verify Copilot remembers context from first prompt

test.expected-result: Copilot maintains conversation context across prompts within a session

test.description: Measure protocol overhead excluding Copilot thinking time

threat.description: Path traversal via Copilot's fs/read_text_file requests could read files outside the intended working directory (e.g., ~/.ssh/id_rsa).

mitigation.description: Resolve all paths and verify they fall within the working directory
mitigation.implementation: Use path.resolve() then verify result starts with the working directory prefix. Reject any path that resolves outside. Log rejected attempts.

threat.description: YOLO mode auto-approves all tool executions by Copilot, which could include destructive operations (rm -rf, git force push, etc.).

mitigation.description: Accepted risk for v1 (user explicitly opted into YOLO). Log all approved permissions with full details for auditability. Document the risk prominently in README.
mitigation.implementation: Log every permission approval with tool kind, description, and affected paths

threat.description: Prompt injection via file contents — if Copilot reads files that contain adversarial instructions, it could be manipulated.

mitigation.description: Out of scope for this server — this is a Copilot-level concern. Our server is a transparent bridge and does not filter prompt content.
mitigation.implementation: Document in README that prompt injection risks are inherited from Copilot

sensitive-data.item: Copilot authentication tokens (managed by Copilot CLI, not by this server)
File contents read from disk and passed to Copilot

encryption.in-transit: Stdio (same machine, no network). TCP mode: localhost only by default.

access-control.authentication: None — relies on Copilot CLI's own authentication
access-control.authorization: Working directory scoping for file access

compliance.requirement: N/A — local developer tool, no user data storage

notes.note: Initial spec created. Awaiting review before implementation.

Research completed: Validated architecture against current MCP ecosystem. - Confirmed pure stdio MCP server via npx is the standard pattern (29K+ servers on npm). - Identified MCACP as existing general-purpose competitor (22 tools, multi-agent). - Our differentiator: focused Copilot-only bridge with simpler tool surface. - Pinned dependency versions: MCP SDK 1.27.1, Zod 3.25.76, TS 5.9.3, Vitest 4.0.18. - Added design decisions D-004 (npx distribution), D-005 (Zod validation), D-006 (permission policy). - Added full dependency manifest and project config (tsconfig, package.json) to spec. - ACP protocol version 2025-09-01 confirmed stable in Copilot public preview.
